question.text: For ATM Options on Futures, the Put-Call parity relationship implies:

answer.text: Call price equals Put price (C = P)
Call price is always greater than Put price
Put price is always greater than Call price
Call price equals twice the Put price

explanation.text: **The Logic:** For Options on Futures ATM, C - P = [F/(1+rt)] - [K/(1+rt)]. When F = K (ATM), C - P = 0, so C = P. **The Trap:** For Options on Goods (where underlying is spot), C > P for ATM because S > PV(K). The C = P relationship is specific to Options on Futures at ATM. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers put-call parity for equity options. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This ATM parity helps verify option pricing — if ATM call and put on futures have significantly different premiums, there may be a mispricing or arbitrage opportunity.
